channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: Critically appraised paper: Mobile-based pulmonary rehabilitation improved exercise capacity as much as and health status more than centre-based pulmonary rehabilitation for chronic obstructive pulmonary disease [synopsis].
item.description: A critically appraised paper found that mobile-based pulmonary rehabilitation improved exercise capacity as much as and health status more than center-based pulmonary rehabilitation for chronic obstructive pulmonary disease patients.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Do blood flow restriction exercises offer additional benefits when compared to conventional exercises in musculoskeletal rehabilitation? A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: This systematic review and meta-analysis of RCTs found that low-load exercise with blood flow restriction (LLE-BFR) was superior to low-load exercise (LLE) and comparable to high-load exercise (HLE) for improving muscle strength, pain, and disability in musculoskeletal conditions.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Frontline agreement in diagnosis and treatment for musculoskeletal dysfunctions. A prospective, randomized, controlled study.
item.description: This pilot RCT at a Swiss hospital ED found high agreement between physiotherapists' and resident physicians' diagnoses, treatment plans, and discharge decisions for musculoskeletal conditions compared to senior physicians, supporting Advanced Physiotherapy Practice (APP) roles.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Development of a Core Patient-Reported Outcome (Measures) Set for Pediatric Physical Therapy.
item.description: A core set of 9 patient-reported outcomes (PROs) and corresponding measures (PROMs) for pediatric physical therapy was developed and grouped into three domains: functioning, participation, and well-being, suitable for daily practice, research, and quality assessment.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Recent highlights in motor recovery and rehabilitation research after stroke.
item.description: This systematic review identified 118 RCTs, protocols, and systematic reviews with meta-analyses published since 2020, highlighting recent advances in motor recovery and rehabilitation research after stroke and providing recommendations for future trials.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Smallest worthwhile effect of anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction compared with non-surgical management: a benefit-harm trade-off study.
item.description: This benefit-harm trade-off study using an online survey with 92 athletes estimated the smallest worthwhile effect of ACL reconstruction plus rehabilitation versus rehabilitation alone for improving knee function and return to sport.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Psychometric Properties of the Trunk Impairment Scale Used After Stroke: A Systematic Review Using COSMIN Methodology.
item.description: A systematic review using COSMIN methodology evaluated the measurement properties of the Trunk Impairment Scale (TIS) in individuals with stroke, clarifying its psychometric properties.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Two-Year Outcomes of the Proximal Hamstring Avulsion Rehabilitation Regimes: Longitudinal Versus Accelerated Protocol (PHARRLAP) Study: A Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: This prospective RCT (n=60) found that an accelerated rehabilitation pathway, compared to a conservative one, for proximal hamstring tendon repair showed comparable clinical outcomes, strength, and functional symmetry, with 2-year follow-up.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Personalized blood flow restriction training at variable occlusion pressures improves multisystem function in overweight and obese older women - European Journal of Applied Physiology
item.description: This research demonstrated that personalized blood flow restriction training at variable occlusion pressures improves multisystem function in overweight and obese older women.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Improved Flexor Spasticity in Patients with Chronic Stroke Using a Dynamic Wrist-Hand Stretching Device: A Pilot Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: A pilot RCT (n=30) found that an 8-week intervention using a dynamic wrist-hand stretching device significantly improved flexor spasticity and hand function in chronic stroke patients with mild-to-moderate spasticity compared to a control group.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Efficacy and Modulating Factors of Repetitive Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation on Motor Function in Parkinson's Disease: A Meta-Analysis.
item.description: This meta-analysis evaluated the short-term and long-term therapeutic effects of repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation (rTMS) on motor function in Parkinson's disease patients, identifying stimulation site, coil type, stimulation frequency, and regimen as modulating factors.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Establishing weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injury: a systematic review and methodological quality analysis.
item.description: This systematic review aimed to establish weighted criteria for return to play (RTP) after hamstring injury, highlighting the current lack of clear consensus and the high recurrence rate of these injuries.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Shortwave diathermy for managing knee osteoarthritis: A systematic review with meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of 17 trials (n=1,372) found that shortwave diathermy (SWD) significantly improved pain (mean -2.13; 95% CI [-2.64, -1.62]; p < .01) and walking ability (mean -0.72; 95% CI [-1.02, -0.43]; p < .01) in patients with knee osteoarthritis.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Patterns of Outcome Measure Utilization in Stroke Rehabilitation Lower Extremity Trials Conducted in Low-to-Middle versus High-Income Countries.
item.description: This review of 1,548 RCTs found that low-to-middle income countries (LMICs) tend to use more generalized impairment and activities of daily living outcome measures that rely on patient observation compared to high-income countries (HICs) in stroke lower extremity motor rehabilitation trials.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Anti-Compensatory Saccades Changes After Visuo-Vestibular Physical Therapy in People With Acute Unilateral Vestibulopathy: A Prospective Observational Study.
item.description: This prospective observational study of 92 AUVP patients found that an 8-week visuo-vestibular physical therapy program improved anti-compensatory saccades (AcS) as assessed by the Suppression Head Impulse Paradigm (SHIMP), suggesting AcS may reflect central compensation.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Prioritising fall prevention strategies in Parkinson's disease: A multi-method evaluation.
item.description: This multi-method study integrated a narrative review, a two-round Delphi process with 15 experts, and multi-criteria decision-making to identify and quantitatively prioritize fall-prevention strategies for people with Parkinson's disease.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Associations of neuropathic pain and pain catastrophizing with rehabilitation outcomes in patients with stroke.
item.description: This prospective observational study explored the associations of neuropathic pain and pain catastrophizing with rehabilitation outcomes in stroke patients, finding potential links to poorer functional recovery and quality of life after a 4-week inpatient rehabilitation program.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Multi-Domain Benefits of Exergaming for Sarcopenia Management in Institutionalized Older Adults: Physical, Psychological, and Social Outcomes.
item.description: A 12-week pragmatic quasi-experimental study involving 72 institutionalized older adults with sarcopenia demonstrated multi-domain benefits (physical, psychological, social) from an exergaming intervention (Nintendo Switch Ring Fit Adventure).
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Sarcopenia Increases Adjacent Segment Degeneration Risk within 3 Years of Anterior Cervical Discectomy and Fusion.
item.description: An exploratory retrospective cohort study of 333 ACDF patients found that sarcopenia was associated with an increased risk of radiographic adjacent segment degeneration within 3 years post-surgery.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Genicular Artery Embolization Using Imipenem/Cilastatin vs. Microsphere for Knee Osteoarthritis (GAUCHO). 12-Month Clinical Results of a Single-blind Randomized Controlled Trial | CardioVascular and Interventional Radiology | Springer Nature Link
item.description: This single-blind randomized controlled trial compared genicular artery embolization using imipenem/cilastatin versus microsphere for knee osteoarthritis, reporting 12-month clinical results.
item.author: Sports Medicine
